all_retrieved_content.page_content: Underpinned by its superior capability in supply chain and manufacturing planning and management, Lotus Technology is also able to offer a wide range of customized options in terms of vehicle designs and functionalities, which are highly appreciated by its customers. Lotus Technology has established a comprehensive charging network and provided hassle-free charging services through at-home charging solutions, on-the-road charging solutions, and 24/7 charging fleets. The ultra charging stations, in particular, provide users with an ultimate charging experience through Lotus Technology's proprietary ultra-fast charging technology developed by Ningbo Viridi. As of December 31, 2023, there were 882 ZEEKR charging stations with different charging capabilities, including 436 ultra charging stations, 330 super charging stations, and 116 light charging stations, covering over 130 cities in China, further supported by over 54 thousand third-party charging stations that cover over 340 cities in China with approximately 610 thousand charging piles in total. Lotus Technology has established in-depth partnerships with a number of internationally renowned smart mobility companies, laying a solid foundation for its business development and global expansion. For example, Lotus Technology collaborates with Mobileye, a subsidiary of Intel and one of its strategic investors, for consumer-ready autonomous driving solutions. Lotus Technology is working with Waymo, a leader in L4 autonomous driving technology, to supply vehicles for the Waymo One Fleet. The vehicles are purpose-built TaaS vehicles based on SEA-M, which is an advanced version of SEA and a high-tech mobility solution that supports a range of future mobility products including robotaxis and logistics vehicles.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Furthermore, Lotus Technology has deep relationships with a range of leading suppliers, such as CATL, Bosch, and Aptiv. In addition, Lotus Technology has a relationship with Onsemi, a leader in intelligent power and sensor technologies. Lotus Technology will be provided with Onsemi’s EliteSiC, its silicon carbide power devices, to enhance the performance, charging efficiency, and driving range for its BEV products. Lotus Technology operates in a rapidly growing market with extensive potential. Driven by improving battery and smart technologies, supportive regulatory policies, and enhancement of charging infrastructure, China’s battery electric vehicle (BEV) market has substantial room for growth in both volume and BEV penetration. China’s BEV sales volume is expected to be approximately five times greater, reaching 13.7 million units in 2028 from 2021, according to Frost & Sullivan. The premium BEV market is expected to experience even faster growth, almost increasing to over seven times the volume in 2021 by 2028, according to Frost & Sullivan. The European BEV market has significant size and growth potential, which is expected to reach 5.3 million units in sales volume in 2028. representing a CAGR of 18.6% from 2024 to 2028, according to Frost & Sullivan. In the future, Lotus Technology also plans to tap into the robotaxi market in the United States. In December 2023, Lotus Technology started to deliver the ZEEKR 001 in Europe.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology's revenue from vehicle sales amounted to RMB1,544.3 million, RMB19,671.2 million, and RMB33,911.8 million (US$94,776.4 million) in 2021, 2022, and 2023, respectively, with a gross profit margin of 1.8%, 4.7%, and 15.0%, respectively. In addition to vehicle sales, Lotus Technology generated revenues from research and development services, other services, and sales of batteries and other components. Lotus Technology's total revenue amounted to RMB6,527.5 million, RMB31,899.4 million, and RMB51,672.6 million (US$7,277.9 million) in 2021, 2022, and 2023, respectively, with a gross profit margin of 15.9%, 7.7%, and 13.3%, respectively. Lotus Technology recorded a net loss of RMB4,514.3 million, RMB7,655.1 million, and RMB8,264.2 million (US$1,164.0 million) in 2021, 2022, and 2023, respectively. Lotus Technology is a fast-growing battery electric vehicle (BEV) technology company. Through developing and offering next-generation premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions, Lotus Technology aspires to lead the electrification, intelligentization, and innovation of the automobile industry. Since its inception, Lotus Technology has focused on innovation and technological advancement in BEV architecture, hardware, software, and application of new technologies. Lotus Technology's efforts are backed by its strong in-house research and development capabilities, high operational flexibility, and flat, efficient organizational structure. Together, these features enable fast product development, launch, and iteration, and a series of customer-oriented products and go-to-market strategies. Thus, Lotus Technology is able to rapidly expand even with a limited operating history.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In particular, Lotus Technology's in-house capabilities are also supported by (i) the Sweden-based research and development center CEVT in the research and development of intelligent mobility solutions, and (ii) Ningbo Viridi, Lotus Technology's PRC subsidiary focused on products and systems relating to batteries, motors, electric control, power solutions, and energy storage. Lotus Technology deploys cutting-edge autonomous driving technology into its battery electric vehicles by world-leading players such as Mobileye and has also announced plans to integrate NVIDIA DRIVE Thor, the 2,000 TOPS AV superchip, into its centralized vehicle computer for the next generation of intelligent battery electric vehicles. title: OVERVIEW 
summary: ZEEKR is an innovative and rapidly expanding battery electric vehicle (BEV) technology company committed to transforming the automobile industry through electrification and advanced technology. all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a fast-growing battery electric vehicle (BEV) technology company developing and offering next-generation premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions to lead the electrification, intelligentization, and innovation of the automobile industry. Lotus Technology is an independently-run startup-style company relying on its in-house research and development (R&D) capabilities and self-owned sales and marketing network, among others. Lotus Technology adopts a flat and efficient organizational structure led by key management with diversified backgrounds. Since inception, Lotus Technology has been managed and directed by its executive officers, and save for Conghui An, who is currently an executive director of Geely Auto, none of Lotus Technology's executive officers are members of management of Geely Auto. Additionally, Mr. An is expected to not hold any positions in Geely Auto prior to or upon the completion of the offering. While Lotus Technology's chairman, Shufu Li, is also the chairman of Geely Auto, upon the completion of the offering, the directors that Lotus Technology shares in common with Geely Auto will not have executive roles at Lotus Technology. Lotus Technology has been dedicated to serving its customers by leveraging top-notch technology, advanced product concepts, and an enriched entrepreneurial spirit that embraces creativity and innovation. ZEEKR is strategically positioned as a premium battery electric vehicle (BEV) brand that delivers an ultimate experience covering driving, charging, after-sale service, and customer community engagement. ZEEKR’s product family meets a wide spectrum of customer needs in different mobility and travel scenarios and is highly customized with a wide selection of vehicle configurations.

metadata.title_summary:
title: An Innovative Automotive Technology Company with Strong Product Offering to Capture Massive Premium BEV Market Opportunity 
summary: ZEEKR is an emerging battery electric vehicle (BEV) technology company dedicated to creating premium electric vehicles and innovative solutions that promote the electrification and intelligentization of the automotive sector. Operating as an independent startup, ZEEKR utilizes its in-house research and development capabilities alongside a proprietary sales and marketing network, all managed by a diverse and efficient leadership team. Notably, while some executives have ties to Geely Auto, they will not hold executive roles post-offering, ensuring a distinct operational identity.

Since its launch less than three years ago, ZEEKR has introduced several models, including the ZEEKR 001, a five-seater crossover, the luxury ZEEKR 009 MPV, and the compact ZEEKR X SUV, among others. The brand has garnered significant market attention, achieving rapid sales milestones, such as 10,000 deliveries of the ZEEKR 001 shortly after its release, positioning it as one of China’s fastest-selling premium BEVs.

ZEEKR vehicles are built on Geely Holding's proprietary SEA platform, which facilitates swift development and launch of new models while ensuring exceptional performance and user experience. The ZEEKR 001 and ZEEKR 009 are recognized for their impressive range capabilities, with the ZEEKR 009 being the first pure-electric MPV to exceed 800 km in range. Additionally, ZEEKR incorporates advanced autonomous driving technologies powered by cutting-edge hardware, enhancing both performance and safety.

The company emphasizes customer satisfaction through a focus on innovative technology and premium features, such as customizable in-vehicle configurations and luxurious amenities in models like the ZEEKR 009 Grand, which includes heated massaging seats and high-quality entertainment systems. Overall, ZEEKR is committed to delivering a holistic smart mobility experience, setting a high standard in the premium BEV market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content:
ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 009, and ZEEKR 001 FR are equipped with 7nm Mobileye EyeQ5H high-performance chips and Falcon Eye Vidar systems, all of which bring out the full potential of ZEEKR’s autonomous driving suite. The upscale sedan model adopts the NVIDIA DRIVE Orin platform to power Lotus Technology's proprietary intelligent autonomous driving systems. In addition, ZEEKR 001 (2024 model) incorporates the latest Mobileye EVO domain control platform, which enables bolstered performance and heightened system stability. • Extensive customization options with fast launch pace enabled by SEA. Lotus Technology offers customers a large number of different setup combinations and customization options. • Maverick driving performance that stands out among its peers. Equipped with industry-leading driving metrics, Lotus Technology's battery electric vehicles (BEVs) hold the leading position in the industry based on key performance metrics, according to Frost & Sullivan. See “Industry Overview — Competitive Landscape.”  
• Premium in-vehicle configurations and distinct exterior design to enhance user experience and meet demands for individuality. Lotus Technology offers drivers and passengers a suite of in-vehicle configurations featuring comfort and pleasure. According to Frost & Sullivan, ZEEKR 001 offers more competitive specifications compared with BEVs of similar price ranges. ZEEKR 001 also embodies a stylish exterior, which is suitable for customers with bold and expressive lifestyles. For instance, ZEEKR 009 Grand offers two separate rear seats, each equipped with electric adjustments, heating and massage functions, ensuring comprehensive comfort for passengers.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a fast-growing BEV technology company. Through developing and offering next-generation premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions, Lotus Technology aspires to lead the electrification, intelligentization, and innovation of the automobile industry. Since its inception, Lotus Technology has focused on innovation in BEV architecture, hardware, software, and application of new technologies. Lotus Technology's efforts are backed by strong in-house R&D capabilities, a deep understanding of products, high operational flexibility, and a flat, efficient organizational structure. Together, these features enable fast product development, launch, and iteration, as well as a series of customer-oriented products and go-to-market strategies. Thus, Lotus Technology is able to rapidly expand even with a limited operating history. Lotus Technology strategically spearheaded the premium intelligent BEV market with unique positioning, featuring a strong sense of technology, in-house R&D capabilities, stylish design, high-caliber performance, and a premium user experience. Lotus Technology's current product portfolio primarily includes ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan model. ZEEKR 001. With an unwavering commitment to its mission, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR 001 in April 2021, a five-seater, cross-over hatchback vehicle model with superior performance and functionality. Targeting the premium BEV market, ZEEKR 001 is Lotus Technology's first vehicle model and the world’s first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake, according to Frost & Sullivan. ZEEKR 001 is also the first mass-produced BEV model with over $1,000 km CLTC range, according to Frost & Sullivan. Lotus Technology began the delivery of ZEEKR 001 in October 2021.

metadata.title_summary:
title: OVERVIEW 
summary: ZEEKR is an emerging leader in the battery electric vehicle (BEV) sector, dedicated to driving the electrification and innovation of the automotive industry through the development of premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions. Since its inception, ZEEKR has focused on advancing BEV architecture, hardware, software, and the integration of new technologies, supported by strong in-house research and development (R&D) capabilities and an efficient organizational structure. This approach enables rapid product development and effective market strategies, facilitating swift expansion despite its relatively short operating history.

The company has made notable progress with its innovative vehicle lineup, which includes the ZEEKR 001, the world's first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake, launched in April 2021, and the luxury ZEEKR 009 MPV, introduced in November 2022. The ZEEKR 001 has achieved significant sales milestones, including delivering over 10,000 units within four months of its launch and surpassing 10,000 monthly deliveries in October 2022. By the end of 2023, ZEEKR had delivered a total of 196,633 vehicles, primarily in China, positioning it as a rapidly growing player in the premium BEV market.

ZEEKR's development is underpinned by the SEA platform, an open-source modular architecture owned by Geely Holding, which allows for a diverse range of vehicle types and rapid integration of advanced technologies. The company benefits from Geely's extensive industry experience, innovative engineering, and strong supply chain management, which enhance product reliability and consumer interest.

In addition to its vehicle offerings, ZEEKR emphasizes a customer-centric approach, utilizing a direct-to-consumer sales model and a robust charging network to enhance the user experience. The company has formed strategic partnerships with leading firms like Mobileye and Waymo to advance autonomous driving technologies and expand its global reach.

The BEV market is poised for significant growth, particularly in China and Europe, with projections indicating a substantial increase in sales by 2028. ZEEKR is well-positioned to capitalize on these opportunities, focusing on next-generation mobility solutions that combine luxury, performance, and advanced technology.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In February 2024, Lotus Technology released an upgraded model of ZEEKR 001, or ZEEKR 001 (2024 model). Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 001 (2024 model) in March 2024. In October 2023, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR 001 FR, its cross-over hatchback vehicle model based on ZEEKR 001. Featuring unique exterior and interior design and proprietary technologies, ZEEKR 001 FR is designed to offer outstanding vehicle performance with various driving modes. Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 001 FR in November 2023. • ZEEKR 009. In November 2022, Lotus Technology launched its second model, ZEEKR 009, a luxury six-seater MPV model providing a comfortable, ultra-luxury mobility experience for both families and business uses. ZEEKR 009 is the world’s first premium MPV based on a pure-electric platform, according to Frost & Sullivan. ZEEKR 009 has enjoyed wide popularity since launch, and Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 009 to its customers in January 2023. In April 2024, Lotus Technology launched ZEEKR 009 Grand, a luxury version of ZEEKR 009 featuring enhanced safety, privacy, and intelligence. Lotus Technology also released ZEEKR MIX, its MPV model, in the same month. • ZEEKR X. In April 2023, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR X, its compact SUV model featuring spacious interior design, advanced technology, and superior driving performance. Lotus Technology began to deliver ZEEKR X in June 2023. • ZEEKR Upscale Sedan Model. In November 2023, Lotus Technology launched its first upscale sedan model targeting tech-savvy adults and families. Powered by 800V architecture and multi-link suspension.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: This is among the fastest delivery growth in the premium BEV market in China, according to Frost & Sullivan. The development of Lotus Technology's BEV models is powered by SEA, a set of open-source, electric and modularized platforms owned by Geely Holding compatible with A segment to E segment, covering sedan, SUV, MPV, hatchback, roadster, pick-up truck, and robotaxi, which have a wheelbase mainly between 1,800 mm to 3,300 mm. Lotus Technology depends on Geely Holding to allow the company to continue to utilize SEA, which is currently the most suitable platform for Lotus Technology. The widely compatible SEA enables robust R&D capabilities, execution efficiency, cost efficiency, and control consistency in the vehicle development process, giving Lotus Technology's BEVs significant competitive advantages in the market. SEA also offers the flexibility to quickly adopt and accommodate the latest and most advanced technology improvements. For example, Lotus Technology was able to equip ZEEKR 009 with CATL’s latest Qilin battery, making ZEEKR 009 the first mass-produced BEV model equipped with Qilin battery, according to Frost & Sullivan. Together with Lotus Technology's proprietary advanced battery solutions and highly efficient electric drive system, ZEEKR 009’s extended range version is the world’s first pure-electric MPV model with an over 800 km CLTC range and the longest all-electric range in the MPV market by the end of February 2024, according to Frost & Sullivan.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: As a premium BEV brand incubated by Geely Group, Lotus Technology inherits unique competitive edges from Geely Group that are developed through years of execution experience at the frontier of the industry, such as innovative and agile engineering capabilities, robust R&D capabilities, deep industry expertise, extreme attention to safety, top-notch professionals, strong supply chain and manufacturing management capabilities, and operational know-how. Geely Group’s powerful and world-class brand equity also echoes product innovation, performance, and reliability in its broad customer base, which, in turn, contributes to the significant consumer interest and demand for the ZEEKR brand. These competitive advantages enable Lotus Technology to quickly incorporate customer needs and concepts into its products and manage the complex operation process to achieve the fast ramp-up of production and deliveries. Lotus Technology also leverages Geely Group’s advanced and well-established manufacturing capacity, which helps retain effective oversight over key steps in procurement, manufacturing, and product quality control with minimal capital outlay. At the same time, Lotus Technology's BEVs are manufactured at the manufacturing plant in Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone owned by Geely Holding (the “ZEEKR Factory”), the manufacturing plant in Chengdu owned by Geely Auto (the “Chengdu Factory”), or the manufacturing plant in Ningbo Beilun District owned by Geely Holding (the “Meishan Factory”), and Geely Holding was Lotus Technology's largest supplier for 2022 and 2023. Furthermore, before the launch of ZEEKR 001, a significant portion of Lotus Technology's revenue has historically been derived from the sales of batteries and other components and research and development services to Geely Group.

In particular, Lotus Technology's in-house capabilities are also supported by (i) the Sweden-based R&D center CEVT in the research and development of intelligent mobility solutions, and (ii) Ningbo Viridi, Lotus Technology's PRC subsidiary focused on the products and systems relating to battery, motor and electric control, power solutions, and energy storage. Leveraging Lotus Technology's in-house E/E Architecture design and operating system, ZEEKR OS, Lotus Technology continuously updates its BEV functions through effective and efficient FOTA. Lotus Technology deploys cutting-edge autonomous driving technology into its BEVs by world-leading players such as Mobileye, and has also announced its plan to integrate NVIDIA DRIVE Thor, the 2,000 TOPS AV superchip, into its centralized vehicle computer for the next generation intelligent BEV. Lotus Technology also offers an intelligent cockpit to deliver interactive, immersive, and enjoyable driving experiences. To successfully achieve its mission, Lotus Technology assembled a top-notch management team with diversified yet complementary backgrounds and experiences. Lotus Technology's management team possesses entrepreneurial spirit, deep automotive and technology sector expertise along with customer-centric operation experience, which are essential to driving the company's future development. Lotus Technology's co-founder and CEO Conghui An has over 25 years of experience in multiple executive management positions in Geely Group and accumulated profound industry insights and senior management experience with an excellent track record.

metadata.title_summary:
title: OVERVIEW 
summary: ZEEKR is an emerging leader in the battery electric vehicle (BEV) sector, dedicated to driving the electrification and innovation of the automotive industry through the development of premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions. Since its inception, ZEEKR has focused on advancing BEV architecture, hardware, software, and the integration of new technologies, supported by strong in-house research and development (R&D) capabilities and an efficient organizational structure. This approach enables rapid product development and effective market strategies, facilitating swift expansion despite its relatively short operating history.

The company has made notable progress with its innovative vehicle lineup, which includes the ZEEKR 001, the world's first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake, launched in April 2021, and the luxury ZEEKR 009 MPV, introduced in November 2022. The ZEEKR 001 has achieved significant sales milestones, including delivering over 10,000 units within four months of its launch and surpassing 10,000 monthly deliveries in October 2022. By the end of 2023, ZEEKR had delivered a total of 196,633 vehicles, primarily in China, positioning it as a rapidly growing player in the premium BEV market.

ZEEKR's development is underpinned by the SEA platform, an open-source modular architecture owned by Geely Holding, which allows for a diverse range of vehicle types and rapid integration of advanced technologies. The company benefits from Geely's extensive industry experience, innovative engineering, and strong supply chain management, which enhance product reliability and consumer interest.

In addition to its vehicle offerings, ZEEKR emphasizes a customer-centric approach, utilizing a direct-to-consumer sales model and a robust charging network to enhance the user experience. The company has formed strategic partnerships with leading firms like Mobileye and Waymo to advance autonomous driving technologies and expand its global reach.

The BEV market is poised for significant growth, particularly in China and Europe, with projections indicating a substantial increase in sales by 2028. ZEEKR is well-positioned to capitalize on these opportunities, focusing on next-generation mobility solutions that combine luxury, performance, and advanced technology.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In addition to ZEEKR, Mr. An has successfully established, developed, and operated both Geely and Lynk&Co, two well-established vehicle brands of Geely Group. Lotus Technology is guided by its customer-oriented principle to provide customers with service and experience in every aspect of their journey with the company. Lotus Technology adopts a customer-oriented direct-to-consumer (DTC) sales model with a focus on innovative and interactive engagement with its customers. Lotus Technology has established extensive customer touchpoints including 24 ZEEKR Centers, 240 ZEEKR Spaces, 31 ZEEKR Delivery Centers, and 45 ZEEKR Houses in China, and two ZEEKR Centers overseas as of December 31, 2023. In addition, Lotus Technology closely interacts with customers by building an integrated online and offline customer community to provide a holistic experience that goes beyond the purchase of intelligent battery electric vehicles (BEVs). Within the ZEEKR APP, customers can enjoy one-stop car purchase, charging solutions, financial services, roadside assistance, intelligent car control, online shopping of ZEEKR lifestyle products, social interaction, and seamless communication with the customer services team. Lotus Technology also holds a variety of offline customer events to nurture a vibrant ZEEKR user community. These customer engagement efforts enable Lotus Technology to better understand customer needs to be incorporated into future product design and continuously strengthen customer loyalty and stickiness. Underpinned by its superior capability in supply chain and manufacturing planning and management, Lotus Technology is also able to offer a wide range of customized options in terms of vehicle designs and functionalities, which are highly appreciated by its customers.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has established a comprehensive charging network and provided hassle-free charging services through at-home charging solutions, on-the-road charging solutions, and 24/7 charging fleets. The ultra charging stations, in particular, provide users with an ultimate charging experience through Lotus Technology's proprietary ultra-fast charging technology developed by Ningbo Viridi. As of December 31, 2023, there were 882 ZEEKR charging stations with different charging capabilities, including 436 ultra charging stations, 330 super charging stations, and 116 light charging stations, covering over 130 cities in China, further supported by over 54 thousand third-party charging stations that cover over 340 cities in China with approximately 610 thousand charging piles in total. Lotus Technology has established in-depth partnerships with a number of internationally renowned smart mobility companies, laying a solid foundation for its business development and global expansion. For example, Lotus Technology collaborates with Mobileye, a subsidiary of Intel and one of its strategic investors, for consumer-ready autonomous driving solutions. Going forward, Lotus Technology will continue to deepen its collaboration with Mobileye. Lotus Technology is working with Waymo, a leader in L4 autonomous driving technology, to supply vehicles for the Waymo One Fleet. The vehicles are purpose-built TaaS vehicles based on SEA-M, which is an advanced version of SEA and a high-tech mobility solution that supports a range of future mobility products including robotaxis and logistics vehicles. Furthermore, Lotus Technology has deep relationships with a range of leading suppliers, such as CATL, Bosch, and Aptiv.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In addition, Lotus Technology has a relationship with Onsemi, a leader in intelligent power and sensor technologies. Lotus Technology will be provided with Onsemi’s EliteSiC, its silicon carbide power devices, to enhance the performance, charging efficiency, and driving range for its BEV products. Lotus Technology operates in a rapidly growing market with extensive potential. Driven by improving battery and smart technologies, supportive regulatory policies, and enhancement of charging infrastructure, China’s battery electric vehicle (BEV) market has substantial room for growth in both volume and BEV penetration. China’s BEV sales volume is expected to be approximately five times greater, reaching 13.7 million units in 2028 from 2021, according to Frost & Sullivan. The premium BEV market is expected to experience even faster growth, almost increasing. to over seven times the volume in 2021 by 2028, according to Frost & Sullivan. The European battery electric vehicle (BEV) market has significant size and growth potential, which is expected to reach 5.3 million units in sales volume in 2028, representing a CAGR of 18.6% from 2024 to 2028, according to Frost & Sullivan. In the future, Lotus Technology also plans to tap into the robotaxi market in the United States. In December 2023, Lotus Technology started to deliver the ZEEKR 001 in Europe. Lotus Technology's revenue from vehicle sales amounted to RMB1,544.3 million, RMB19,671.2 million, and RMB33,911.8 million (US$4,776.4 million) in 2021, 2022, and 2023, respectively, with a gross profit margin of 1.8%, 4.7%, and 15.0%, respectively.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In addition to vehicle sales, Lotus Technology generated revenues from research and development services, other services, and sales of batteries and other components. Lotus Technology's total revenue amounted to RMB6,527.5 million, RMB31,899.4 million, and RMB51,672.6 million (US$7,277.9 million) in 2021, 2022, and 2023, respectively, with a gross profit margin of 15.9%, 7.7%, and 13.3%, respectively. Lotus Technology recorded a net loss of RMB4,514.3 million, RMB7,655.1 million, and RMB8,264.2 million (US$1,164.0 million) in 2021, 2022, and 2023, respectively. Lotus Technology is a fast-growing battery electric vehicle (BEV) technology company. Through developing and offering next-generation premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions, Lotus Technology aspires to lead the electrification, intelligentization, and innovation of the automobile industry. Since its inception, Lotus Technology has focused on innovation and technological advancement in BEV architecture, hardware, software, and application of new technologies. Lotus Technology's efforts are backed by its strong in-house research and development capabilities, high operational flexibility, and flat, efficient organizational structure. Together, these features enable fast product development, launch, and iteration, and a series of customer-oriented products and go-to-market strategies. Thus, Lotus Technology is able to rapidly expand even with a limited operating history. • ZEEKR 001. With an unwavering commitment to its mission, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR 001 in April 2021, a five-seater, cross-over hatchback vehicle model with superior performance and functionality. Targeting the premium BEV market, ZEEKR 001 is Lotus Technology's first vehicle model and the world’s first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake, according to Frost & Sullivan.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In November 2023, Lotus Technology launched its first upscale sedan model targeting tech-savvy adults and families. Powered by 800V architecture and multi-link suspension structure, Lotus Technology's upscale sedan model is expected to achieve a $2.84 s 0-100 km/h acceleration and a $688 km maximum CLTC range. Lotus Technology began the delivery of its first upscale sedan model in January 2024. As a testament to the popularity of Lotus Technology's current vehicle models and its capabilities, Lotus Technology has achieved a total delivery of 10,000 units of ZEEKR 001 in less than four months after the initial delivery, which, according to Frost & Sullivan, is one of the fastest among the major mid- to high-end new energy vehicle (NEV) models. premium BEV models in China. In October 2022, Lotus Technology delivered 10,119 units of ZEEKR 001 to the market, making it the first pure-electric premium vehicle model manufactured by a Chinese BEV brand with over 10,000 units of single-month delivery volume, according to Frost & Sullivan. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 196,633 ZEEKR vehicles since the first vehicle delivery in October 2021, including 192,441 delivered in China. This is among the fastest delivery growth in the premium BEV market in China, according to Frost & Sullivan.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: As a premium BEV brand incubated by Geely Group, Lotus Technology inherits unique competitive edges from Geely Group that are developed through years of execution experience at the frontier of the industry, such as innovative and agile engineering capabilities, robust research and development capabilities, deep industry expertise, extreme attention to safety, top-notch professionals, strong supply chain and manufacturing management capabilities, and operational know-how. Geely Group’s powerful and world-class brand equity also echoes product innovation, performance, and reliability in its broad customer base, which, in turn, contributes to the significant consumer interest and demand for the ZEEKR brand. These competitive advantages enable Lotus Technology to quickly incorporate customer needs and concepts into its products and manage the complex operation process to achieve the fast ramp-up of production and deliveries. Lotus Technology also leverages Geely Group’s advanced and well-established manufacturing capacity, which helps retain effective oversight over key steps in procurement, manufacturing, and product quality control with minimal capital outlay. At the same time, Lotus Technology's BEVs are manufactured at the ZEEKR Factory, the Chengdu Factory, and the Meishan Factory, which are owned and operated by Geely Group, and Geely Holding was Lotus Technology's largest supplier for 2022 and 2023. Furthermore, before the launch of ZEEKR 001, a significant portion of Lotus Technology's revenue has historically been derived from sales of batteries and other components and research and development services to Geely Group. Lotus Technology has strong in-house technological capabilities focusing on electrification and intelligentization.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has entered into Cooperation Framework Agreements with Geely Group for the manufacturing of the ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, and ZEEKR 009 at the ZEEKR Factory, the manufacturing of the ZEEKR X at the Chengdu Factory, and the manufacturing of the upscale sedan model at the Meishan Factory. Lotus Technology's future operation and prospects depend on the successful ramping and maintaining of operations in the ZEEKR Factory, the Chengdu Factory, and the Meishan Factory. See “Business — Manufacturing and Quality Control.” There can be no assurance that Lotus Technology's oversight on battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturing will always be effective, as the ZEEKR Factory, the Chengdu Factory, and the Meishan Factory are not owned by Lotus Technology. Vehicle production at such factories may also experience delays or suspensions. For example, vehicle production at the ZEEKR Factory was temporarily suspended in early 2022 due to the COVID-19 pandemic, and Lotus Technology also encountered a temporary suspension in production at the ZEEKR Factory in the first quarter of 2023 due to production line upgrades at the factory. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology has not experienced any material customer complaints caused by the delays or suspensions of vehicle production at the ZEEKR Factory, the Chengdu Factory, and the Meishan Factory. In addition, there will be a negative impact on Lotus Technology's business operation if, in the future, such original equipment manufacturer (OEM) model is limited by applicable laws and regulations, or becomes subject to more stringent regulatory oversight.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Any problems or delays in ramping and maintaining operations of the ZEEKR Factory, the Chengdu Factory or the Meishan Factory could negatively affect the production of our BEVs. 
summary: The company has entered into Cooperation Framework Agreements with Geely Group for the production of its ZEEKR models, with specific models allocated to different factories: the ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, and ZEEKR 009 will be produced at the ZEEKR Factory, the ZEEKR X at the Chengdu Factory, and an upscale sedan at the Meishan Factory. The company's future success relies heavily on the effective ramp-up and operation of these facilities. However, the oversight of battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturing at these non-owned factories has faced challenges, including production delays and suspensions due to COVID-19 and necessary upgrades. While customer complaints have been minimal, potential future regulatory changes and the inherent complexities of BEV manufacturing could pose risks to the company's operations and financial health. Further information is available in the "Business — Manufacturing and Quality Control" section.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Given the size and complexity of the manufacturing of BEVs, it is possible that the ZEEKR Factory, the Chengdu Factory, or the Meishan Factory may experience issues or delays in further expanding their production output. If any of these factories experiences any such issues or delays, Lotus Technology's business, prospects, operating results, and financial condition could be adversely impacted.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Any problems or delays in ramping and maintaining operations of the ZEEKR Factory, the Chengdu Factory or the Meishan Factory could negatively affect the production of our BEVs. 
summary: The company has entered into Cooperation Framework Agreements with Geely Group for the production of its ZEEKR models, with specific models allocated to different factories: the ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, and ZEEKR 009 will be produced at the ZEEKR Factory, the ZEEKR X at the Chengdu Factory, and an upscale sedan at the Meishan Factory. The company's future success relies heavily on the effective ramp-up and operation of these facilities. However, the oversight of battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturing at these non-owned factories has faced challenges, including production delays and suspensions due to COVID-19 and necessary upgrades. While customer complaints have been minimal, potential future regulatory changes and the inherent complexities of BEV manufacturing could pose risks to the company's operations and financial health. Further information is available in the "Business — Manufacturing and Quality Control" section.

Lotus Technology began the delivery of its first upscale sedan model in January 2024. Lotus Technology's current and future BEV models will define the company's success. Going forward, Lotus Technology plans to capture the extensive potential of the premium BEV market globally through an expanding portfolio of vehicles. For instance, Lotus Technology plans to launch vehicles for the next generation of mobility lifestyles. Through these future models, Lotus Technology intends to provide premium mobility solutions characterized by innovation, comfort, and intelligence, as well as a spacious and luxurious high-tech experience with enhanced performance. As a testament to the popularity of Lotus Technology's current products and capabilities, Lotus Technology has achieved a total delivery of 10,000 units of ZEEKR 001 in less than four months after the initial delivery, which, according to Frost & Sullivan, is one of the fastest among the major mid- to high-end NEV models and premium BEV models in China. In October 2022, Lotus Technology delivered 10,119 units of ZEEKR 001 to the market, making it the first pure-electric premium vehicle model manufactured by a Chinese BEV brand with over 10,000 units of single-month delivery volume, according to Frost & Sullivan. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 196,633 ZEEKR vehicles since the first vehicle delivery in October 2021, including 192,441 delivered in China.

metadata.title_summary:
title: OVERVIEW 
summary: ZEEKR is an emerging leader in the battery electric vehicle (BEV) sector, dedicated to driving innovation and electrification within the automotive industry. The company focuses on developing next-generation premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions, leveraging strong in-house research and development capabilities and a flexible organizational structure to rapidly create and launch customer-oriented products. 

Since its inception, ZEEKR has introduced several groundbreaking models, including the ZEEKR 001, recognized as the world's first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake with a range exceeding 1,000 km, and the luxury ZEEKR 009 MPV, which offers an ultra-luxury experience. The lineup also features the compact ZEEKR X SUV and a newly launched upscale sedan model, all designed to cater to diverse consumer needs while emphasizing stylish design, high performance, and exceptional user experiences.

ZEEKR's growth trajectory has been impressive, with significant milestones such as delivering over 10,000 units of the ZEEKR 001 within four months of its launch and achieving a total of 196,633 vehicle deliveries by the end of 2023. The company benefits from its affiliation with Geely Group, which provides extensive industry expertise, innovative engineering, and a robust supply chain, enhancing ZEEKR's product reliability and market competitiveness.

The company is also committed to advancing its technological capabilities, focusing on electrification and intelligent mobility. This includes the development of its proprietary E/E architecture and ZEEKR OS, which facilitate over-the-air updates and the integration of cutting-edge autonomous driving technologies. 

As the global BEV market is poised for substantial growth, particularly in China and Europe, ZEEKR aims to capitalize on this potential by expanding its vehicle portfolio and enhancing its customer engagement through a direct-to-consumer sales model. The company has established a comprehensive charging network and formed strategic partnerships to bolster its business development and global reach, positioning itself for continued success in the rapidly evolving electric vehicle landscape.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a fast-growing BEV technology company. Through developing and offering next-generation premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions, Lotus Technology aspires to lead the electrification, intelligentization, and innovation of the automobile industry. Since its inception, Lotus Technology has focused on innovation in BEV architecture, hardware, software, and application of new technologies. Lotus Technology's efforts are backed by strong in-house R&D capabilities, a deep understanding of products, high operational flexibility, and a flat, efficient organizational structure. Together, these features enable fast product development, launch, and iteration, as well as a series of customer-oriented products and go-to-market strategies. Thus, Lotus Technology is able to rapidly expand even with a limited operating history. Lotus Technology strategically spearheaded the premium intelligent BEV market with unique positioning, featuring a strong sense of technology, in-house R&D capabilities, stylish design, high-caliber performance, and a premium user experience. Lotus Technology's current product portfolio primarily includes ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan model. • ZEEKR 001. With an unwavering commitment to its mission, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR 001 in April 2021, a five-seater, cross-over hatchback vehicle model with superior performance and functionality. Targeting the premium BEV market, ZEEKR 001 is Lotus Technology's first vehicle model and the world’s first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake, according to Frost & Sullivan. ZEEKR 001 is also the first mass-produced BEV model with over $1,000 km CLTC range, according to Frost & Sullivan. Lotus Technology began the delivery of ZEEKR 001 in October 2021.

Lotus Technology also offers an intelligent cockpit to deliver interactive, immersive, and enjoyable driving experiences. To successfully achieve Lotus Technology's mission, Lotus Technology assembled a top-notch management team with diversified yet complementary backgrounds and experiences. Lotus Technology's co-founder and CEO Conghui An has over 25 years’ experience in multiple executive management positions in Geely Group and accumulated profound industry insights and senior management experience with an excellent track record.

Lotus Technology is guided by its customer-oriented principle to provide customers with service and experience in every aspect of their journey with the company. Lotus Technology adopts a customer-oriented direct-to-consumer (DTC) sales model with a focus on innovative and interactive engagement with its customers. Lotus Technology also holds a variety of offline customer events to nurture a vibrant ZEEKR user community. Lotus Technology's customer engagement efforts enable the company to better understand customer needs to be incorporated into future product design and continuously strengthen customer loyalty and stickiness. Underpinned by its superior capability in supply chain and manufacturing planning and management, Lotus Technology is also able to offer a wide range of customized options in terms of vehicle designs and functionalities, which are highly appreciated by its customers.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has established a comprehensive charging network and provided hassle-free charging services through at-home charging solutions, on-the-road charging solutions, and 24/7 charging fleets. The ultra charging stations, in particular, provide users with an ultimate charging experience through Lotus Technology's proprietary ultra-fast charging technology developed by Ningbo Viridi. As of December 31, 2023, there were 882 ZEEKR charging stations with different charging capabilities, including 436 ultra charging stations, 330 super charging stations, and 116 light charging stations, covering over 130 cities in China, further supported by over 54 thousand third-party charging stations that cover over 340 cities in China with approximately 610 thousand charging piles in total. Lotus Technology has established in-depth partnerships with a number of internationally renowned smart mobility companies, laying a solid foundation for Lotus Technology's business development and global expansion. For example, Lotus Technology collaborates with Mobileye, a subsidiary of Intel and one of Lotus Technology's strategic investors, for consumer-ready autonomous driving solutions. Going forward, Lotus Technology will continue to deepen its collaboration with Mobileye. Lotus Technology is working with Waymo, a leader in L4 autonomous driving technology, to supply vehicles for the Waymo One Fleet. The vehicles are purpose-built TaaS vehicles based on SEA-M, which is an advanced version of SEA and a high-tech mobility solution that supports a range of future mobility products including robotaxis and logistics vehicles. Furthermore, Lotus Technology has deep relationships with a range of leading suppliers, such as CATL, Bosch, and Aptiv.

The European BEV market has significant size and growth potential, which is expected to reach 5.3 million units in sales volume in 2028, representing a CAGR of 18.6% from 2024 to 2028, according to Frost & Sullivan. In the future, Lotus Technology also plans to tap into the robotaxi market in the United States. In December 2023, Lotus Technology started to deliver the ZEEKR 001 in Europe. all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has in the past entered into a series of cooperation framework agreements in relation to battery electric vehicle (BEV) development and manufacturing with Geely Group. The cooperation framework agreements relate to either the manufacturing of the Zeekr 001, Zeekr 001 FR, and Zeekr 009 at the Zeekr Factory (the “Zeekr Factory Cooperation Framework Agreements”), the manufacturing of the Zeekr X at the Chengdu Factory (the “Chengdu Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement”), the manufacturing of the upscale sedan model and Zeekr 7X at the Meishan Factory (the “Meishan Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement”), as well as the manufacturing of the Zeekr Mix and vehicles for Waymo at the Chunxiao Factory (the “Chunxiao Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement,” and together with the Zeekr Factory Cooperation Framework Agreements and the Chengdu Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement, the “Cooperation Framework Agreements”). The currently effective Zeekr Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement was entered into in August 2022. The currently effective Chengdu Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement was entered into in February 2023. The currently effective Meishan Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement was entered into in January 2024. The currently effective Chunxiao Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement was entered into in May 2024. Lotus Technology purchases vehicles from Geely Group at a price made up of the purchase cost of direct materials and a preagreed markup. Lotus Technology provides a standard product warranty to the vehicle purchasers. Under the Cooperation Framework Agreements, in the event where product quality issues are due to product manufacturing, Lotus Technology is entitled to seek damages from the factories, as applicable.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Cooperation Framework Agreements 
summary: The company has formed several cooperation framework agreements with Geely Group to develop and manufacture battery electric vehicles (BEVs) across various factories. These agreements include the production of models such as the Zeekr 001, Zeekr 009, and Zeekr X at designated factories, with specific agreements signed between August 2022 and May 2024. The company purchases vehicles from Geely at a price based on direct material costs plus a markup, and it provides a standard warranty to buyers. The agreements also include provisions for addressing product quality issues, allowing the company to claim damages from factories for manufacturing defects or raw material supplier issues. Additionally, they establish a mechanism for reserved annual production capacity, with compensation arrangements for any discrepancies between actual and reserved production levels. The agreements have varying expiration dates, with some set to expire in 2025 and 2027, while others do not have specified terms. Early termination can occur under certain conditions, such as insolvency or failure to meet obligations.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has entered into Cooperation Framework Agreements with Geely Group for the manufacturing of the ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, and ZEEKR 009 at the ZEEKR Factory, the manufacturing of the ZEEKR X at the Chengdu Factory, and the manufacturing of the upscale sedan model at the Meishan Factory. Lotus Technology's future operation and prospects depend on the successful ramping and maintaining of operations in the ZEEKR Factory, the Chengdu Factory, and the Meishan Factory. See “Business — Manufacturing and Quality Control.” There can be no assurance that Lotus Technology's oversight on battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturing will always be effective, as the ZEEKR Factory, the Chengdu Factory, and the Meishan Factory are not owned by Lotus Technology. Vehicle production at such factories may also experience delays or suspensions. For example, vehicle production at the ZEEKR Factory was temporarily suspended in early 2022 due to the COVID-19 pandemic, and Lotus Technology also encountered a temporary suspension in production at the ZEEKR Factory in the first quarter of 2023 due to production line upgrades at the factory. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology has not experienced any material customer complaints caused by the delays or suspensions of vehicle production at the ZEEKR Factory, the Chengdu Factory, and the Meishan Factory. In addition, there will be a negative impact on Lotus Technology's business operations if, in the future, such original equipment manufacturer (OEM) model is limited by applicable laws and regulations, or becomes subject to more stringent regulatory oversight.

metadata.title_summary:

summary: The company has entered into Cooperation Framework Agreements with Geely Group to produce various models, including the ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 009, and ZEEKR X, across multiple factories. The success of these operations relies heavily on the effective management and continuity of production at the ZEEKR, Chengdu, and Meishan factories, which are not owned by the company. Past challenges, such as the temporary shutdown of the ZEEKR Factory due to the COVID-19 pandemic and subsequent production line upgrades, highlight potential vulnerabilities in manufacturing oversight. Customer complaints related to production delays further underscore these concerns. Additionally, future regulatory constraints on OEM models and the inherent complexities of manufacturing Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs) pose risks that could negatively impact the company's operational performance and financial health. For further insights, please refer to the "Business — Manufacturing and Quality Control" section.

all_retrieved_content.page_content:
Lotus Technology has in the past entered into a series of cooperation framework agreements in relation to battery electric vehicle (BEV) development and manufacturing with Geely Group. The cooperation framework agreements relate to either the manufacturing of the ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, and ZEEKR 009 at the ZEEKR Factory (the “ZEEKR Factory Cooperation Framework Agreements”), the manufacturing of the ZEEKR X at the Chengdu Factory (the “Chengdu Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement”), as well as the manufacturing of the upscale sedan model at the Meishan Factory (the “Meishan Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement,” and together with the ZEEKR Factory Cooperation Framework Agreements and the Chengdu Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement, the “Cooperation Framework Agreements”). The currently effective ZEEKR Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement was entered into in August 2022. The currently effective Chengdu Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement was entered into in February 2023. The currently effective Meishan Factory Cooperation Framework Agreement was entered into in January 2024. Under the currently effective Cooperation Framework Agreements, Lotus Technology is responsible for: • supervising vehicle development with respect to production targets, costs, project progress, and quality control; • implementing supply chain management, such as supplier selection, material pricing, and business negotiation, to secure sufficient and timely supply; • conducting research and development with respect to battery electric vehicles (BEVs); • promoting vehicle sales; and  
• paying expenses in relation to trial and testing during the vehicle production and manufacturing process. On the other hand, Geely Group is responsible for: • manufacturing vehicles at the ZEEKR Factory and the Chengdu Factory;

metadata.title_summary:
title: Cooperation Framework Agreements 
summary: The company has formed several cooperation framework agreements with Geely Group to enhance the development and manufacturing of battery electric vehicles (BEVs). These agreements cover the production of various models, including the ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan, across different factories, with key agreements signed between August 2022 and January 2024. The agreements delineate responsibilities for both parties, emphasizing collaboration, compliance with standards, and effective communication.

The company is tasked with overseeing vehicle development and production management, ensuring adherence to production targets, cost management, quality control, and supply chain efficiency. This includes selecting suppliers, negotiating prices, and managing expenses related to trials and testing. Geely Group, on the other hand, focuses on vehicle design, manufacturing, and distribution, with a commitment to innovation and sustainability in electric and hybrid vehicle development.

Operational responsibilities at the ZEEKR and Chengdu factories include regulatory compliance, material inspection, quality assurance, and maintaining high manufacturing standards. The company also provides warranties and manages indemnification for product-related issues, with mechanisms in place to address manufacturing defects and supplier-related claims.

The agreements stipulate annual production capacity requirements and outline compensation for discrepancies between actual and reserved capacity. The ZEEKR Factory agreement expires on December 31, 2025, the Chengdu Factory agreement on December 31, 2027, and the Meishan Factory agreement has no specified expiration. Early termination conditions include insolvency, changes in control, mutual consent, or failure to meet obligations. Overall, these frameworks underscore a commitment to quality, compliance, and consumer protection in vehicle manufacturing.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Within less than three years since ZEEKR’s inception, Lotus Technology has launched multiple commercialized electric vehicle models, namely ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan model. ZEEKR 001 is a five-seater crossover shooting brake BEV model targeting the premium market and mainly addressing the customer need for practical yet stylish travel. Lotus Technology released ZEEKR 001 (2024 model) in February 2024 and started vehicle delivery in March 2024. ZEEKR 009 is a luxury six-seater MPV addressing the customer need for luxury mobility. In April 2023, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR X, the compact SUV model, and began to deliver ZEEKR X in June 2023. Lotus Technology also started to deliver ZEEKR 001 FR in November 2023 and began to deliver the first upscale sedan model in January 2024. In addition to major upgrades such as the release of ZEEKR 001 (2024 model), Lotus Technology has also been continuously fine-tuning and improving the performance of its other BEV models. For example, Lotus Technology updated the interior configurations of ZEEKR 009 and improved the chassis system of ZEEKR X in 2024. The products have been well received by the market, where Lotus Technology has achieved a total delivery of 10,000 units of ZEEKR 001 in less than four months since its initial delivery in October 2021, which, according to Frost & Sullivan, is one of the fastest among the major mid- to high-end new energy vehicle (NEV) models and premium BEV models in China.

metadata.title_summary:
title: An Innovative Automotive Technology Company with Strong Product Offering to Capture Massive Premium BEV Market Opportunity 
summary: We are an innovative battery electric vehicle (BEV) technology company dedicated to developing premium BEVs and advanced solutions that drive the electrification and intelligentization of the automotive sector. As an independent startup, we utilize our in-house R&D and proprietary sales and marketing networks, supported by a diverse management team that operates independently from Geely Auto, aside from our chairman, Shufu Li. Our commitment to exceptional customer service is reflected in our cutting-edge technology and entrepreneurial spirit.

ZEEKR, our premium BEV brand, aims to provide an outstanding experience in driving, charging, after-sale service, and community engagement. Since our launch less than three years ago, we have introduced several models, including the ZEEKR 001, 001 FR, 009, and ZEEKR X, each designed to meet various customer needs. The ZEEKR 001 targets the premium market with its stylish crossover design, while the luxurious ZEEKR 009 serves as a six-seater MPV. The ZEEKR X, a compact SUV, debuted in April 2023, followed by an upscale sedan model in January 2024. Our vehicles have achieved remarkable market success, with the ZEEKR 001 becoming one of the fastest-selling premium BEVs in China, delivering 10,000 units within four months.

Future ZEEKR models will utilize Geely Holding's proprietary SEA platform, ensuring rapid development and launch capabilities while offering outstanding performance and stylish designs. Our vehicles, including the ZEEKR 001 and 009, are recognized for their impressive battery range, with the ZEEKR 001 achieving up to 750 km and the ZEEKR 009 exceeding 800 km, making it the world's first pure-electric MPV to do so. Additionally, we emphasize advanced autonomous driving features, integrating high-performance Mobileye technology across our models.

ZEEKR vehicles are designed for comfort and enjoyment, with extensive customization options and premium in-vehicle configurations. The ZEEKR 001 stands out in its class, offering competitive specifications and a bold design that appeals to discerning customers. Overall, ZEEKR is committed to delivering a comprehensive smart mobility experience, positioning itself as a leader in the BEV market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: RELATED-PARTY TRANSACTIONS, page 173 
summary: The summary encompasses several key requests and disclosures related to financial reporting and corporate governance for a company engaged in transactions with Geely Group and its affiliates. 

1. **Filing of Agreements**: The company is asked to include all agreements related to transactions with Geely Group, particularly those concerning the acquisition and majority ownership of Ningbo Viridi, as well as any product sales agreements. This request aligns with Item 601(b)(10) of Regulation S-K, emphasizing the need for thorough compliance in the registration statement.

2. **Exclusive Forum Provision**: The summary highlights the exclusive forum provision, which designates a specific jurisdiction for legal disputes, aiming to streamline litigation and reduce costs for shareholders. It also notes potential legal risks associated with this provision, including challenges to its enforceability and implications for shareholder rights.

3. **Risk Factors**: A discussion of risk factors from the disclosure document outlines potential legal and operational risks, including market conditions and regulatory changes that could affect the company's performance.

4. **Financial Statements and Accounting Policies**: The summary touches on the need for clarity regarding the accounting treatment of recall costs, particularly following a significant recall by Geely. It emphasizes the importance of formal accounting policies for managing such financial implications. Additionally, it mentions the company's internal R&D capabilities and requests disclosure of accounting policies related to the development costs of proprietary technologies.

5. **Segment Reporting**: The inquiry includes a request for detailed revenue information from the Viridi Segment, which manufactures batteries for the ZEEKR 001 models. This includes quantifying revenue from battery packs sold on an OEM basis, highlighting the materiality of this information for financial reporting.

Overall, the summary provides a comprehensive overview of the company's obligations regarding disclosures, accounting policies, and the implications of its business transactions with Geely Group, ensuring transparency and compliance with regulatory standards.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology's results of operations and financial conditions substantially depend on the sales of its battery electric vehicles (BEVs). Therefore, the delivery volume of the ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan model, which are Lotus Technology's mass-produced BEV models, is a key indicator used to monitor the company's business operations and performance. ZEEKR vehicles are well received by the market driven by their superior performance, stylish design, and functionality that meets diversified customer needs and provides an outstanding mobility experience. In October 2022, Lotus Technology delivered 10,119 units of the ZEEKR 001 to the market, making the ZEEKR 001 the first pure-electric premium vehicle model manufactured by a Chinese BEV brand with over 10,000 units of single-month delivery volume, according to Frost & Sullivan. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 196,633 ZEEKR vehicles since the first vehicle delivery in October 2021, including 192,441 delivered in China. This is among the fastest deliveries in the premium BEV market in China, according to Frost & Sullivan. The following chart summarizes the monthly deliveries of ZEEKR vehicles for the periods indicated.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Monthly Vehicle Deliveries 
summary: The financial and operational success of ZEEKR is closely tied to the sales of its battery electric vehicles (BEVs), particularly key models like the ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan. These models are vital indicators of the company's performance, with delivery volumes serving as benchmarks for market success. ZEEKR has achieved notable market acceptance, attributed to the vehicles' impressive performance, stylish design, and versatility, which meet various customer needs.

In October 2022, ZEEKR marked a significant achievement by delivering over 10,000 units of the ZEEKR 001 in a single month, becoming the first Chinese BEV brand to reach this milestone. By the end of 2023, the company had delivered a total of 196,633 vehicles since its launch in October 2021, with the majority of these sales occurring in China, positioning ZEEKR among the fastest-growing brands in the premium BEV sector.

Monthly delivery data reveals trends and fluctuations in ZEEKR's performance, with a notable increase in deliveries over time. For instance, March 2024 saw deliveries rise to 13,012 units after a dip in February, while 2023 experienced steady growth, peaking at 13,476 units in December. Seasonal factors, such as winter and the Chinese New Year, typically affect delivery volumes, as evidenced by the decline in early 2024. However, ZEEKR's ability to adapt to market demands and production challenges highlights its resilience and growth trajectory in the competitive BEV market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Zeekr 009, the world’s first premium pure-electric MPV, was launched in November 2022, with deliveries beginning in January 2023. In April 2024, Zeekr introduced the Zeekr 009 Grand, a luxury four-seater flagship MPV featuring enhanced safety, privacy, and intelligence. The Zeekr 009 Grand is built on an $800 \mathrm{V}$ electrical system, powered by a 5C Qilin Battery, and features dual Qualcomm Snapdragon 8295 chips for an intelligent front and rear cabin experience. The latest Zeekr 009 in 2024 is available in six-seater and seven-seater configurations, offering industry-leading safety, comfort, performance, and intelligence. The vehicle features a one-piece high-strength aluminum rear-end structure, achieving a CNCAP 5-star safety rating.

summary: The Zeekr 009, which debuted in November 2022 as the first premium pure-electric MPV, began its deliveries in January 2023. This model features an 800V electrical system and is equipped with a 5C Qilin Battery, along with dual Qualcomm Snapdragon 8295 chips to enhance the cabin experience. all_retrieved_content.page_content:
Lotus Technology's second BEV model, ZEEKR 009, is a luxury six-seater MPV featuring a comfortable mobility experience. ZEEKR 009 was officially launched on November 1, 2022, following which Lotus Technology began taking reservations. ZEEKR 009 enjoyed wide popularity since launch, and Lotus Technology commenced delivery in January 2023. Targeted at families and business groups, the ZEEKR 009 is purposely designed to deliver safety, comfort, and durability. • Safety. The vehicle body of the ZEEKR 009 is made of one-piece high-strength rear-end aluminum to effectively absorb impacts in a collision. As a testament to its safety, the ZEEKR 009 is the first MPV model that passed the frontal center column collision test in the world, according to Frost & Sullivan. Similar to the ZEEKR 001, the ZEEKR 009 uses leading driving assistance technologies to ensure improved driving safety. For details, see “— Our Competitive Strengths — Industry-leading Technology and R&D Capabilities Supporting Product Leadership and Fast Iteration.”   
• Comfort. The ZEEKR 009 provides passengers with complete comfort and relaxation during the trip. With a wheelbase of $^ { 3 , 2 0 5 \mathrm { m m } }$ and a width of $2 , 0 2 4 ~ \mathrm { m m }$, the ZEEKR 009 holds six leather chairs in a spacious vehicle cabin of approximately 7.4 square meters. Passengers can also enjoy a variety of entertainment on the road with the ceiling-mounted 15.6-inch screen and intelligent in-cabin interaction system. Chairs in the first two rows are also equipped with a massage function.

metadata.title_summary:
title: ZEEKR 009 
summary: The ZEEKR 009, launched on November 1, 2022, is a luxury six-seater MPV designed for families and business groups, focusing on safety, comfort, and performance. Following its launch, it quickly gained popularity, leading to reservations and deliveries starting in January 2023. 

Safety features include a one-piece high-strength aluminum body that effectively absorbs impacts, making it the first MPV globally to pass the frontal center column collision test. It also incorporates advanced driving assistance technologies akin to those in the ZEEKR 001. 

In terms of comfort, the ZEEKR 009 boasts a spacious cabin of approximately 7.4 square meters with six luxurious leather chairs, a 15.6-inch ceiling-mounted screen, and an intelligent in-cabin interaction system. The vehicle is equipped with an air suspension system that adjusts ground clearance for optimal comfort, and the first two rows of seats feature massage functionality.

Performance-wise, the ZEEKR 009 is powered by CATL’s Qilin battery, offering an impressive range of over 800 km (CLTC) and a dual electric motor system that delivers a maximum power of 400 kW and torque of 686 N·m, achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 4.5 seconds. 

Connectivity features include a 10.25-inch instrument cluster, an AI Mate smart assistant, and a premium YAMAHA audio system. The vehicle’s intelligence is enhanced by a Mobileye EyeQ5H chip and the Falcon Eye Vidar system for improved vision and an advanced autonomous driving experience.

Since its introduction, the ZEEKR 009 has generated substantial revenue, accounting for 17.2% of the company's total revenue in its first year. In April 2024, ZEEKR expanded its lineup with the ZEEKR 009 Grand, a luxury variant that emphasizes enhanced safety, privacy, and intelligence, featuring a robust electrical system and advanced processing capabilities for a superior driving experience. Overall, the ZEEKR 009 exemplifies the brand's commitment to innovation in electric mobility and intelligent driving technologies, appealing to a premium market with its luxurious offerings.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: • The ZEEKR 009 can be equipped with (i) $140 \mathrm{kW h}$ Qilin battery packs or (ii) $116 \mathrm{kW h}$ battery packs, both developed by CATL, depending on different vehicle versions. The ZEEKR 009 was the first mass-produced BEV model equipped with CATL’s Qilin battery, which gives the ZEEKR 009 an $822 \mathrm{km}$ maximum CLTC range. • The ZEEKR X is equipped with $66 \mathrm{kW h}$ battery packs, which enable the ZEEKR X a $560 \mathrm{km}$ or $512 \mathrm{km}$ maximum CLTC range depending on different vehicle versions. The battery packs developed by Ningbo Viridi can also be enhanced by Ningbo Viridi's proprietary battery management system, or BMS, to manage and optimize the battery performance, durability, and safety. For details of the battery solutions and technologies of Ningbo Viridi, see “— Ningbo Viridi.”

metadata.title_summary:
title: Battery Solutions 
summary: ZEEKR vehicles are designed with a focus on reliable battery solutions that emphasize safety, efficiency, and durability. The ZEEKR 001 model offers versatile battery options, including 100 kWh and 86 kWh packs from CATL or Ningbo Viridi, with the 100 kWh version featuring Ni55 cells that provide a maximum CLTC range of 741 km. This battery can be charged from 10% to 80% in just 30 minutes, and a quick five-minute charge allows for about 120 km of travel. The 2024 model introduces 100 kWh or 95 kWh Qilin battery packs, with the 100 kWh version achieving a range of 750 km and enabling 210 km of travel with a five-minute charge. The ZEEKR 001 FR enhances performance further with an 800 V Qilin battery for improved range and charging speed.

The ZEEKR 009 offers larger battery options of 140 kWh or 116 kWh Qilin packs, also from CATL, achieving a remarkable maximum CLTC range of 822 km, marking it as the first mass-produced battery electric vehicle (BEV) utilizing this technology. Meanwhile, the ZEEKR X is equipped with a 66 kWh battery pack, providing a maximum CLTC range of 560 km or 512 km, depending on the version. Additionally, Ningbo Viridi's proprietary battery management system (BMS) enhances the performance, durability, and safety of its battery packs. For further details on Ningbo Viridi's innovations, refer to the relevant section on their technologies.

Lotus Technology began the delivery of ZEEKR 001 in October 2021. In February 2024, Lotus Technology released an upgraded model of ZEEKR 001 (2024 model). Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 001 (2024 model) in March 2024. In October 2023, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR 001 FR, its cross-over hatchback vehicle model based on ZEEKR 001. Featuring unique exterior and interior design and proprietary technologies, ZEEKR 001 FR is designed to offer outstanding vehicle performance with various driving modes. Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 001 FR in November 2023. • ZEEKR 009. In November 2022, Lotus Technology launched its second model, ZEEKR 009, a luxury six-seater MPV model providing a comfortable, ultra-luxury mobility experience for both families and business uses. ZEEKR 009 is the world’s first premium MPV based on a pure-electric platform, according to Frost & Sullivan. ZEEKR 009 has enjoyed wide popularity since launch, and Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 009 to its customers in January 2023. In April 2024, Lotus Technology launched ZEEKR 009 Grand, a luxury version of ZEEKR 009 featuring enhanced safety, privacy, and intelligence. Lotus Technology also released ZEEKR MIX, its MPV model, in the same month. • ZEEKR X. In April 2023, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR X, its compact SUV model featuring spacious interior design, advanced technology, and superior driving performance. Lotus Technology began to deliver ZEEKR X in June 2023. • ZEEKR Upscale Sedan Model.

metadata.title_summary:
title: OVERVIEW 
summary: ZEEKR is an emerging leader in the battery electric vehicle (BEV) sector, dedicated to driving innovation and electrification within the automotive industry. The company focuses on developing next-generation premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions, leveraging strong in-house research and development capabilities and a flexible organizational structure to rapidly create and launch customer-oriented products. 

Since its inception, ZEEKR has introduced several groundbreaking models, including the ZEEKR 001, recognized as the world's first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake with a range exceeding 1,000 km, and the luxury ZEEKR 009 MPV, which offers an ultra-luxury experience. The lineup also features the compact ZEEKR X SUV and a newly launched upscale sedan model, all designed to cater to diverse consumer needs while emphasizing stylish design, high performance, and exceptional user experiences.

ZEEKR's growth trajectory has been impressive, with significant milestones such as delivering over 10,000 units of the ZEEKR 001 within four months of its launch and achieving a total of 196,633 vehicle deliveries by the end of 2023. The company benefits from its affiliation with Geely Group, which provides extensive industry expertise, innovative engineering, and a robust supply chain, enhancing ZEEKR's product reliability and market competitiveness.

The company is also committed to advancing its technological capabilities, focusing on electrification and intelligent mobility. This includes the development of its proprietary E/E architecture and ZEEKR OS, which facilitate over-the-air updates and the integration of cutting-edge autonomous driving technologies. 

As the global BEV market is poised for substantial growth, particularly in China and Europe, ZEEKR aims to capitalize on this potential by expanding its vehicle portfolio and enhancing its customer engagement through a direct-to-consumer sales model. The company has established a comprehensive charging network and formed strategic partnerships to bolster its business development and global reach, positioning itself for continued success in the rapidly evolving electric vehicle landscape.
